title: AI-Powered Cinematic Universe Platform enGEN3 Launched
text:
Oct 9, 2025 12:00am PT
AI-Powered Cinematic Universe Platform enGEN3 Launched by Goldfinch, The Squad (EXCLUSIVE)
By
Naman Ramachandran
Plus Icon
Naman Ramachandran
Latest
Hamaguchi’s French Debut ‘All of a Sudden’ Stuns Cannes With 7-Minute Ovation, Longest of the Fest So Far
4 hours ago

enGEN3
Two entertainment companies are betting that artificial intelligence can help independent filmmakers compete with major studios in building expansive story worlds.
Goldfinch
and
The Squad
have unveiled
enGEN3
, a jointly owned platform that uses
AI
to help creators develop cinematic universes while managing IP rights and revenue sharing with fans. The venture aims to democratize tools typically available only to large studios, enabling filmmakers to expand stories across multiple media formats.
Related Stories
Isabelle Huppert on the Mystery of Asghar Farhadi’s 'Parallel Tales,' Cannes and Why 'Hollywood Remains Hollywood'
Eight Directors, Five Countries, Four Films: Inside KawanKawan Media's 'Next Step Studio Indonesia' at Cannes
“enGEN3 gives filmmakers studio-level tools to build cinematic universes with fan participation, extending their stories seamlessly across film, games, print, and beyond,” said Jordan Bayne, founder of The Squad and enGEN3.
Popular on Variety
The platform’s first project is “By Blood & By Bone,” a sci-fi anime feature pilot from writer Dave Kebo. The film integrates two Web3 anime collections – Ordinauts on Bitcoin and Oni Force on Ethereum – in what the companies describe as a new model for community-driven storytelling.
“With ‘By Blood & By Bone,’ we’re bringing community storytelling into an epic anime series while creating a model for IP ownership and monetization that benefits both creators and fans,” said Kebo.
Henry Finn, CEO of Oni Force, added: “This is the next chapter in storytelling: ownership and imagination shared by all.”
The venture taps experienced tech and creative talent. CTO Doug Thompson previously held roles at gaming and AI companies including Remedy and Sojen. Creative director HaZ Dulull has directed for Disney+ and contributed to video game projects including “Dune: Awakening” and Fortnite. He runs transmedia studio Beyond The Pixels.
A Q1 2026 full launch is targeted for enGEN3, with beta access coming sooner. The companies plan to roll out more projects and tools next year, drawing from Goldfinch’s catalog of hundreds of financed productions.
“At Goldfinch, we back bold visions for the future of entertainment,” said Phil McKenzie, co-founder of Goldfinch. “Jordan pioneered the Film3 model, and enGEN3 represents the next evolution – a way for creators and fans to connect through both creation and distribution.”
Goldfinch has backed more than 300 projects since its 2014 founding and has won Academy Awards and BAFTAs. The Squad focuses on projects blending cinema with generative AI and Web3 technology under what it calls the Film3 framework – emphasizing creator ownership and audience participation in storytelling.
